senses.definition: in relation to or following surgery
examples: The patient was monitored postsurgically for signs of infection.
Postsurgically, she showed gradual improvement.

senses.definition: situated after or relating to the symphysis region (anatomical joint area) in studies or descriptions
examples: Postsymphysial changes were noted in the imaging study.
The researchers referenced postsymphysial anatomy in their analysis.

senses.definition: relating to the neuron or cell after a synapse in neural transmission
examples: Postsynaptic receptors respond to neurotransmitters.
Postsynaptic effects can influence signal strength.

senses.definition: in a postsynaptic manner; in relation to the cell after a synapse
examples: The signal was amplified postsynaptically.
Postsynaptically, receptor activation occurs after neurotransmitter release.

senses.definition: a term that may refer to a process or feature labeled 'post-sync'; used in computing or timing contexts to denote a sync event after an initial synchronization
examples: The device triggers a postsync operation after data capture.
We observed a delay during postsync in the protocol.

senses.definition: having completed or undergone a postsync process
examples: The data were postsynced to the server.
Postsynced files were saved to the archive.

senses.definition: the act of performing a postsync operation; syncing after an initial synchronization
examples: The system is postsyncing after the capture run.
We are postsyncing the log with the main database.

senses.definition: instances of postsync operations or events
examples: The logs show multiple postsyncs during the transfer.
Each postsyncs entry corresponds to a sync after the initial step.

senses.definition: relating to the sacral part after a synaptic reference; a highly technical term possibly used in specialized anatomy or neuroscience contexts
examples: Postsynsacral measurements were recorded in the study.
The term appears in the advanced anatomical table.

senses.definition: pertaining to or resulting from syphilis after the initial infection; used in medical contexts
examples: Postsyphilitic lesions were observed during examination.
The patient had postsyphilitic complications.

senses.definition: occurring after systole in a medical or physiological context
examples: Postsystolic murmurs were detected on auscultation.
The image showed postsystolic relaxation phases.

senses.definition: relating to or following tabes dorsalis or tabetic symptoms; used in historical medical contexts
examples: Posttabetic features were noted in the patient history.
The term appears in old medical literature on neurosyphilis.

senses.definition: located behind the tarsal region of the eyelid or foot in specialized contexts
examples: Posttarsal markings were described in the anatomical section.
The posttarsal area was examined by the clinician.

senses.definition: occurring after taxonomic classification or after tax in a given context
examples: Posttax adjustments were applied to the dataset.
Posttax criteria define subsequent steps.

senses.definition: a person who is older than a teen but still a young adult
examples: Most postteens are finishing college or starting jobs.
Postteen years can be a time of exploration.

senses.definition: plural of postteen; several people who are past adolescence
examples: The event was attended by several postteens from the community.
Postteens often seek internships and early career opportunities.

senses.definition: occurring after the temple region or after temporal events; used in specialized contexts
examples: Posttemporal measurements were reported by the researchers.
The term appears in the advanced anatomy text.

senses.definition: the act or state of applying tension after an initial setup, often used in engineering or construction
examples: Posttensioning was used to reinforce the concrete slab.
The posttension system improved structural integrity.

senses.definition: having undergone posttensioning; reinforced by posttensioning
examples: A posttensioned slab provides greater load capacity.
Posttensioned cables were embedded in the beam.

senses.definition: the process of applying tension to reinforcement after initial placement
examples: Posttensioning improves the resistance to cracking.
The contractor performed posttensioning to meet design specs.

senses.definition: plural of posttensionion; refers to multiple posttensioning actions or systems
examples: The project includes several posttensions to support the structure.
We inspected the posttensions for any slack.

senses.definition: a test conducted after an initial test or intervention
examples: The participants took a posttest to measure learning gains.
Posttests showed improvement compared to pretests.

senses.definition: plural of posttest; tests after an intervention
examples: Teachers reviewed the posttests for progress.
Posttests are used to evaluate effectiveness.

senses.definition: occurring after a tetanus event or tetanic stimulation; used in medical contexts
examples: Posttetanic responses were recorded in the electrophysiology study.
The posttetanic phase followed the stimulation.

senses.definition: relating to the thalamus region after a reference point; used in neuroanatomy
examples: Postthalamic pathways were mapped in the study.
The postthalamic area is referenced in imaging data.
